assertions.title: MIC resolves through USDT with USDC as terminal token

steps.expected: price > 0 with trade_path showing USDT hop and USDC as terminal output_token
steps.observed: price=0.08382922086218535 (>0 ✓); trade_path has one step: source='<y.prices.dex.uniswap.uniswap.UniswapMultiplexer object at 0x7ffff4d910d0>' (Python object repr, not a string), input_token=0x368B...E51, output_token='USD' (NOT USDC address). No USDT hop visible. Response time ~79 seconds.
cached=true, trade_path=null (trade_path is not persisted in cache)

assertions.issues:
Two issues detected:
1) The trade_path does NOT show the expected multi-hop routing through USDT with USDC as terminal output_token. Instead it shows a single step with output_token='USD' (the string literal, not the USDC address 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48).
2) The 'source' field in the PriceStep is set to the Python object representation of the UniswapMultiplexer (str(dex)), not a clean string name. This is because getattr(dex, '__name__', str(dex)) falls back to str(dex) since UniswapMultiplexer lacks __name__.

ROOT CAUSE: V2.get_price() returns a raw UsdPrice float, not a PriceResult with multi-hop steps. The internal USDT→USDC path extension happens inside get_quote() (getAmountsOut call) but is not exposed as separate PriceStep objects. The magic.py wrapper wraps this as a single step with output_token='USD'.

NOTE: The price value itself (0.08382922) is correct and positive — the V2 fix IS working (MIC resolved without hanging, using USDT→USDC path under the hood). Only the trade_path representation fails the assertion.

assertions.title: MIC lookup via server API returns multi-hop trade path

steps.expected: trade_path array showing multi-hop resolution: MIC→USDT→USDC (at minimum two hops with USDC as terminal)
steps.observed: trade_path contains exactly one step: {source: '<UniswapMultiplexer object>', input_token: '0x368B...', output_token: 'USD', pool: null, price: 0.084}. No USDT intermediate hop. No USDC terminal token address.

assertions.issues: Same root cause as VAL-STABLE-004: the V2 UniswapV2Pool.get_price() method returns a UsdPrice float, not a PriceResult with individual hop steps. The USDT→USDC atomic multi-hop path IS executed via getAmountsOut internally, but this is not reflected as separate PriceSteps in the returned trade_path. The magic.py layer collapses it to a single step with output_token='USD'. To pass this assertion, the V2 pricing code would need to return a PriceResult with multiple PriceStep objects (one for MIC→USDT, one for USDT→USDC), or the single step would need to use the USDC address as output_token and include the pool address.

frictions.description: Cold-start MIC request took ~79 seconds (first warm-up request on freshly rebuilt container). Previous test run reportedly hung for 10+ minutes; the O(1) getPair fix resolved the hang but cold start is still slow due to V2 pool discovery and RPC calls.
frictions.resolution: Request completed within the 120s max-time window. Used --max-time 120 as instructed.

frictions.description: Cached response returns trade_path=null. The server caches only the price value, not the trade_path, so the detailed routing info is only available on the first (uncached) response.
frictions.resolution: Used the first uncached response for trade_path evaluation. Evidence file captured the live response.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: both FAIL. The MIC price resolves successfully (0.083 USD) without hanging, confirming the O(1) getPair fix works. However, the trade_path in the response does NOT expose the multi-hop USDT→USDC routing as separate PriceStep objects — both assertions require the path to explicitly show USDT as intermediate and USDC address as terminal output_token. The V2 multi-hop path is used internally (in getAmountsOut) but collapsed to a single step at the magic.py level with output_token='USD'. Additionally, the 'source' field shows a Python object repr instead of a clean string. These are trade_path serialization/representation issues — the underlying pricing fix IS working correctly.
